attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Fracture of vertebral column (candidate for acute vertebral fracture / structural spinal lesion)
amitriptyline (candidate for the central-neuromodulator class option)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-PAIN-03-00216 — SEQ derived from source CFE-016; ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-PAIN-03 bucket.

Specialty: case_id carries the taxonomy code PAIN (Pain Medicine); the 00.specialty_tags schema enum lacks PAIN, so tags use the closest allowed values MSK/NEURO/MH (id is an opaque partition key and may legitimately differ from case_metadata per taxonomy id_rule). Maintainer to confirm.

Difficulty inferred as red_herring_laden (03): the alarming crisis presentation (total-body pain, near-complete immobility, panic, tachycardia) is a red herring over a negative acute red-flag screen — a deliberate over-triage-resistance test. Baseline tier T2, minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T2.

Age and sex NOT stated in the source note. Schema requires demographics.age and demographics.sex_at_birth, so age was inferred as 42 (mid-adult) and sex_at_birth as female — both are unsupported placeholder inferences forced by the required fields; clinician review to confirm or correct.

Telehealth reprojection applied: source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who examined (hyperesthesia to light touch, paraspinal spasm, no bony step-off, intact motor strength and reflexes), measured vitals (BP 140/90, HR 105, RR 20, Temp 36.8, SpO2 99, Wt 76 kg), observed clear urine without myoglobinuria, and administered oral diazepam. Vitals, exam findings and the urine observation sealed into 10/11 as clinician-obtained; the presentation layer de-anchored to a pre-diagnosis patient account.

Central neuromodulator is recommended as a drug-class option (amitriptyline / SNRI / gabapentinoid): the source note says only to 'optimise chronic neuro-modulators' without naming a specific agent or dose, so NO specific dose is synthesised (dose_route_frequency omitted for that item). Diazepam 5 mg once and celecoxib 200 mg daily are the only doses stated in the source.
Magnesium and low-dose THC/medicinal cannabis are named in the source with no dose stated; no dose synthesised (dose_route_frequency omitted).

Candidate codes are best-effort pending terminology-server verification. AMT candidates for celecoxib (116086008) and amitriptyline (372665008 — placeholder for the neuromodulator-class option) are low-confidence and flagged for verification; magnesium and medicinal-cannabis AMT/SNOMED codes were omitted rather than guessed. ICD-10-AM M79.7 used for fibromyalgia.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
